genre_refs: Emanuel Leutze, 'Washington Crossing the Delaware' (1851); patriotic Americana
description: A straight reproduction of Emanuel Leutze's famous 1851 oil painting 'Washington Crossing the Delaware' (credited in the tweet), posted by the Department of Labor as a Christmas/Revolutionary-War tribute. The painting depicts General George Washington standing heroically at the prow of a small boat crowded with Continental soldiers rowing through ice-choked water at dawn; a soldier holds the early American flag aloft, and other boats follow in the misty background. The image is the artwork itself with NO added on-image text, slogans, seals, or graphic-design overlays, so it is not a designed meme/graphic; the messaging lives entirely in the tweet caption ('Christmas Night, 1776. Never forget the sacrifices your forefathers made for freedom, Patriots.'). Intent: heroic patriotic nostalgia and civil-religion framing of the founding generation. Marked is_designed=false because the asset is an unaltered fine-art repost rather than an agency-designed graphic.
